title: How to debug process that malfunctions within podman
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

I wanted to run openvpn in podman container and initially I thought it's running without any problem (on host `netstat` was listing port as mapped by podman). However when I looked at `netstat` output from within the container then noticed that nothing is listening to network traffic.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Do below as root (ideally should also work when executed as rootless)

1. `podman run -it --rm archlinux /bin/bash`

2. `pacman -Sy openvpn`

3. Run `openvpn` with valid config and certs

**Describe the results you received:**

Openvpn starts and does not report any error however running `netstat -ntlp` from within the container yields no result.

**Describe the results you expected:**

I'd expect openvpn port listed within `netstat` output

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

I'd like to debug this further but I reached my limits. Process I run executes without errors though it does not "listen" to the network traffic. Are there any tricks I can use to see where is the process actually failing? I can drop here zip package with example certs if requested.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.7.0
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.13.5
Git Commit:         b7ce1157b00af09f4a09e39b377aa3abff46ee05-dirty
Built:              Sat Jan 11 13:49:39 2020
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
pacman -Q podman
podman 1.7.0-1
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

Running on my laptop but also seen the same behavior on my throwaway test system on Linode.

comments.body: I have tried to test the container with `--privileged` option and also with `--network="host"` option and run it as root. If I install `openssh` within the container and run it I can see the `sshd` process is listening for traffic on port 22, `openvpn` starts and definitely binds to `tun` device, however it does not listen on expected port (I cannot see `openvpn` entry in `netstat` output).
Are you running rootless or rootfull?

Running as root:

```
podman run --rm -d --name openvpn --network="host" --privileged -p 37898:37898 -v ./entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh archlinux /bin/bash /entrypoint.sh
```

(`entrypoint.sh` attached as `entrypoint.sh.txt`, all certs and keys had been created for this test)

[entrypoint.sh.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4184384/entrypoint.sh.txt)

I see the same behavior when running within alpine container:

```
podman run --rm -d --name openvpn --network="host" --privileged -p 37898:37898 -v ./entrypoint.sh:/entrypoint.sh alpine:3.10 /bin/sh /entrypoint.sh
```

[entrypoint.sh.txt](https://github.com/containers/libpod/files/4184411/entrypoint.sh.txt)
@grzegorzk, can you try with Podman v1.8.0?

It works, though I changed so many things on the way that I'm not sure if it was podman version bump. I will do little writeup on openvpn wiki pages for others to use (https://community.openvpn.net/openvpn/wiki/UnprivilegedUser#RunOpenVPNwithinunprivilegedpodmancontainer). Thanks for your great help!
